articles.title: Laxative-laced brownies rattle Nantucket School Committee meeting
articles.description:
“It’s my understanding they do have a non-detect level of Ex-Lax in them, but I figured since we’re OK with a non-detect level of PFAS, it would probably be OK.”

The post Laxative-laced brownies rattle Nantucket School Committee meeting appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Nantucket School Committee meeting disrupted by brownies containing Ex-Lax, raising health concerns.
Committee members discussed non-detect levels of Ex-Lax, similar to PFAS standards.
Local residents express worry over safety and transparency in school food policies.

Felony charge could lead to significant legal repercussions and community trust issues.

articles.title: Canton issues statement after texts from former sergeant included in Karen Read’s new lawsuit
articles.description:

articles.title: Park Service orders removal of ‘woke’ quotes at Boston’s Bunker Hill Monument
articles.description:
A visitor complaint prompted a review of quotes that are anti-war, pro-immigrant or highlight American hypocrisy on slavery ahead of the monument’s 251st anniversary celebration.

The post Park Service orders removal of ‘woke’ quotes at Boston’s Bunker Hill Monument appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Park Service mandates removal of quotes deemed 'woke' at Bunker Hill Monument.
Review initiated due to a visitor complaint ahead of the monument's 251st anniversary.
Local residents may see changes in the monument's messaging on social issues.

articles.title: ‘Old Thom,’ iconic solitary orca, spotted by Cape Cod fishermen
articles.description:
The orca, recognizable because of its unique dorsal fin, is periodically seen swimming alone off the Cape and Islands.

The post ‘Old Thom,’ iconic solitary orca, spotted by Cape Cod fishermen appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Cape Cod fishermen spotted 'Old Thom,' a solitary orca known for its unique dorsal fin.

articles.title: Mass. House approves later bar hours, outdoor drinking zones for World Cup
articles.description:
The House unanimously approved a summer pilot that would let cities and towns extend alcohol service to 3 a.m. and create designated outdoor drinking districts from June 8 through July 31.

The post Mass. House approves later bar hours, outdoor drinking zones for World Cup appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Massachusetts House approved extending bar hours to 3 a.m. for World Cup.
Pilot program runs from June 8 to July 31, 2026.
Cities can create outdoor drinking zones, boosting local tourism and economy.

articles.title: ‘It’s not done yet,’ Karen Read warns on ‘Today’ show after filing explosive new lawsuit
articles.description:
“I couldn’t just go back to life as it was; I have to continue fighting for justice. The acquittal is deserved, but the wrongs have not been completely righted.”

The post ‘It’s not done yet,’ Karen Read warns on ‘Today’ show after filing explosive new lawsuit appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Karen Read filed a new lawsuit seeking justice after her acquittal.
Read emphasized the need for continued advocacy on the 'Today' show.
Boston residents may see ongoing legal developments affecting local justice perceptions.
